definition: The most common civil burden of proof, requiring that a claim be more likely true than not—essentially, greater than a 50% probability. In Alabama's Atkins proceedings, this means the defendant must show it is more probable than not that his true IQ is 70 or below.
examples: Alabama argues Smith failed to meet his preponderance burden because no method of analyzing his five scores shows it is more likely than not his IQ is under 70.
